title: Vortexa Freight Weekly
summary:
Main
By Dylan Simpson
ANALYSIS / EAST OF SUEZ / DIRTY

Flat demand to keep VLCC rates subdued, but what about congestion? Weekly VLCC average waiting times in Shandong Province vs VLCC availability in the Middle East (RHS)
Refineries in China (and other Asian countries) have commenced their planned refinery maintenance schedule, and departures of crude towards China are set to decline for a second month in April and VLCC tanker demand towards China has remained flat. This has kept VLCC rates on the decline, despite an uptick in VLCC congestion in the Shandong Province as authorities carry out lab checks on crude cargoes.

children.title: Ice class vessels not used for most Baltic Urals voyages
section_path: Ice class vessels not used for most Baltic Urals voyages
children.section_path_text: Ice class vessels not used for most Baltic Urals voyages
children.summary:
Post-ban Baltic Urals voyages by total fleet and ice class capability (LHS) vs. ice class share (RHS)
Leading up to the EU ban on Russian crude, there were doubts that Russia would be able to get Urals exports out of the Baltic due to the size of the ice class fleet that was available.
